dependencies: Certain raw and packaging materials purchased from single-source suppliers or a limited number of suppliers
Over 60% of sales volume delivered by in-house manufacturing, remaining 40% by third-party suppliers
Reliance on third-party manufacturers for certain key products such as Tylenol and Zyrtec
Principal raw materials include resins, silicon, pulp and corn derivatives, paper, agrochemicals, vegetable oils, and oleochemicals
risks: Volatility in cost or availability of raw materials due to military conflicts
Inflationary pressures impacting costs of raw materials, packaging components, and other inputs

Disruptions to manufacturing or supplier operations due to regulatory action, quality control issues, labor disputes, natural disasters, cybersecurity incidents, epidemics, pandemics, geopolitical events
Concentration risk from single-source suppliers or limited number of suppliers for certain materials
Third-party manufacturers may not meet manufacturing requirements or allocate sufficient capacity
Supply chain disruptions from Russia-Ukraine War
Global shipping, logistics, transport and warehousing constraints

regulatory_risks: FDA regulations on OTC drug products, cosmetics, and medical devices
New or more stringent laws or regulations globally
Modernization of Cosmetics Regulation Act expanding FDA authority over cosmetics
Medical Device Regulation (MDR) in EU requiring re-review of products
REACH regulations in EU and similar chemical regulations in other jurisdictions
Privacy and data protection laws (EU GDPR, UK GDPR, China PIPL, US state laws)
Anti-corruption laws (FCPA, UK Bribery Act, Chinese anti-corruption laws)
Environmental, health, and safety laws and regulations
OECD Pillar Two minimum tax regulations
Trade controls including tariffs, sanctions, quotas, trade barriers
FDA proposed order to remove phenylephrine from OTC monograph
Regulatory status changes for pseudoephedrine products in EU countries
Climate change regulations and ESG-related legal requirements
Product labeling and claims regulations (FTC, FDA, EU directives)
Tax law changes including TCJA, GILTI provisions, state tax changes

sanction_risks: Export controls and financial sanctions against Russia, Belarus, and specific areas of Ukraine due to Russia-Ukraine War
Sanctions imposed by US Treasury Department's Office of Foreign Assets Control
Trade embargoes and restrictions on doing business in certain countries
Rising geopolitical trade tensions in key markets such as between US, Western Europe, and China
Potential new or increased tariffs on various products
